title: BEM Methodology
summary: BEM is a way to name your CSS classes clearly.
It stands for Block, Element, Modifier.
The names show how the parts relate.

sections.text: As stylesheets grow, vague class names turn into a mess. BEM is a naming system that keeps them clear, so anyone can look at a class and understand what it is and where it belongs.

sections.title: Block, Element, Modifier
sections.text: BEM breaks names into three ideas. A block is a standalone piece, like a card. An element is a part inside it, like the card's title. A modifier is a variation, like a featured card. The name itself shows the relationship.

sections.title: How the Names Look
sections.text: BEM uses a clear pattern so the three parts are easy to spot in a class name. You can tell at a glance that one class is the block, another is an element inside it, and another is a modified version. There is no guessing about what belongs to what.

media.alt: A class name split into its block, element, and modifier parts

sections.title: Naming Rooms in a House
sections.text: Imagine labeling a house. The block is the kitchen. An element is the kitchen sink, clearly part of the kitchen. A modifier is the kitchen sink that is broken, a special version of it. The names alone tell you the room, the part, and its state.

media.alt: Labels on a kitchen, then its sink, then a broken version of the sink

sections.title: Naming with BEM
items: Find the standalone piece and name it the block.
Name each part inside it as an element of that block.
Add a modifier name for any variation.
Keep the same pattern across the whole project.
Now any class name explains itself.

sections.text: BEM became popular partly because it works in plain CSS with no tools at all. It is just a discipline of naming, which is why teams could adopt it gradually.

terms.definition: A class naming system, short for Block Element Modifier.
A standalone piece, like a card.
A part inside a block, like its title.
A variation of a block or element.
An agreed pattern for naming things.

questions.explanation: BEM stands for Block, Element, Modifier.

options: A standalone piece, like a card
questions.explanation: A block is a standalone piece, like a card.

options: Keeping class names clear and organized
questions.explanation: BEM keeps class names clear and organized in big stylesheets.
